metadata.title: The Next Craft — Hackathon by Crafter Station × Next
metadata.description: From zero to product in 12 hours. In-person hackathon in Lima, Bogotá and Guatemala — July 25, 2026. 120 seats, teams of 3–5, prizes TBA. Apply by July 10.

bootLoader.circular: THE NEXT CRAFT · LOADING · THE NEXT CRAFT · LOADING ·

hero.circular: LIMA · BOGOTÁ · GUATEMALA · 25 JUL 2026 · 12 HOURS · ZERO TO PRODUCT ·

about.headline:
Not an event.
12 hours of building.
about.paragraph1: The Next Craft is the in-person hackathon by Crafter Station × Next. 12 hours of real work, running simultaneously in Lima, Bogotá and Guatemala, with 120 hackers solving real problems.
about.paragraph2: Admission is selective. No room for spectators, no space for pretty slides. You come to ship real product: with users, with data, with something that works when the clock runs out.

tracks.label: TRACKS
tracks.headline:
3 TRACKS.
PICK YOUR WEAPON.
tracks.intro: The bar is still one: what you build has to be used by someone real before the bell rings.

items.name: CONTENT MACHINE
items.desc: Build tools that help create, edit, publish, repurpose or distribute content. For creators, founders, students, communities, small businesses or builders who document what they do.
ideas: Voice notes → LinkedIn posts, TikToks, tweets or newsletters
Build-in-public assistant: your GitHub commits → social updates
Content calendar generator for small businesses
Long videos → short clips, captions, hooks and thumbnails
AI that helps students share what they learned in short-form
items.why: This is how products grow today. And it lets non-technical profiles shine: storytelling, growth, design, distribution.

items.name: OUT OF THE BOX
items.desc: Build something weird, experimental, playful, surprising or impossible to categorize. It can be useful, funny, artistic, chaotic — or all of the above. The one rule: make people react.
ideas: A game where you fight your procrastination monster
A really weird voice interface to manage your tasks
Browser extension that roasts your tabs
Your calendar turned into a Spotify-Wrapped-style story
A 'joke but useful' app for hackathon teams
items.why: Viral projects come from here. Permission to create instead of building another productivity SaaS.

items.name: LEARNING BY SHIPPING
items.desc: Build tools that help people learn faster by creating, practicing, testing themselves or getting feedback. No passive education: the product has to make the user do.
ideas: An AI tutor that teaches with projects, not lectures
Practice interviews for scholarships, internships or jobs
Flashcards that adapt to what you forget
Assignments with Socratic feedback instead of answers
'Learn to code by building clones' assistant
Mentor matching for students and young builders
items.why: The education track, but with a better angle: less school, more agency.

events.description: Mentorship with Next Fellow and Crafter Station
Demos — 3 minutes per team, live product

prizes.label: PRIZES
perks: Access to the Crafter Station community
prizes.note: Jury evaluates with live demo + pitch. We'll publish the rubric soon.

items.question: Who can apply?
items.answer: Anyone who builds: devs, designers, PMs. Admission is selective: what you've shipped matters, not your résumé.

items.question: Can I apply without a team?
items.answer: Yes. Team formation happens at the kickoff. Teams of 3 to 5.

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: Laptop, charger and energy. Food, coffee and power are on us.

items.question: Can I use AI?
items.answer: Yes, you can use AI. But the code must be built during the hackathon — no pre-existing code will be accepted.

items.question: Who owns the IP?
items.answer: You do. 100%. We just want to watch you ship.

items.question: Is it in-person?
items.answer: Yes, it'll be in-person in Lima, Bogotá or Guatemala — whichever hub fits you. No remote option.

items.question: How does the jury evaluate?
items.answer: Live demo + pitch. We'll publish the rubric soon.

finalCta.headline:
DO YOU BUILD?
APPLY.
finalCta.specsSr: Deadline: July 10, 2026, 23:59 GMT-5. 120 seats, selective admission. Free. The form is 7 questions, 90 seconds.
finalCta.whatsappMessage: Hi, I want to apply to The Next Craft
